authors: Shangyuan Wu Department of Communications and New Media
Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
raw_title: Is TikTok News Less “News-Like”? Investigating News Organizations’ Stories on TikTok and Their Alignment/ Divergence with Traditional News Values, Norms, Routines, and Roles
title_en: Is TikTok News Less “News-Like”? Investigating News Organizations’ Stories on TikTok and Their Alignment/ Divergence with Traditional News Values, Norms, Routines, and Roles
title_zh: TikTok新闻是否更“不像新闻”？调查新闻机构在TikTok上的故事及其与传统新闻价值观、规范、惯例和角色的对齐/分歧
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了新闻机构在TikTok上发布的内容是否与传统新闻的标准有所不同。它分析了这些内容如何符合或偏离传统的新闻价值观、工作方式和角色，帮助理解社交媒体如何改变新闻的呈现方式。
plain_summary_en: This study examines whether news content posted by news organizations on TikTok differs from traditional news standards. It analyzes how this content aligns with or diverges from traditional news values, practices, and roles, helping to understand how social media is changing news presentation.
research_direction_zh: 数字新闻与社交媒体平台的内容适应性和规范性研究，关注新闻机构在新媒体环境下的转型和内容策略。
research_direction_en: Research on content adaptation and norms in digital journalism and social media platforms, focusing on the transformation and content strategies of news organizations in new media environments.
